summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reminding the congregation that everyone has a story about God's glory. He began by contrasting the explosive growth of the early church in the Book of Acts with the severe persecution its members faced, noting that the gospel spread most powerfully under pressure. Pastor Travis then connected this to the present day, urging believers not to take their freedom to worship for granted and to recognize that Christians around the world still face persecution for their faith. The core of the message focused on why every Christian must share their personal testimony. Using Acts 4 as a foundation, where Peter and John declared they 'cannot help speaking about what we have seen and heard,' Pastor Travis argued that a genuine encounter with Jesus creates an unstoppable compulsion to tell others. He shared his own testimony of transformation as the driving force behind his ministry. The sermon provided three key reasons to share one's story: it holds the power to set people free from spiritual bondage, it brings healing to others by turning our past pain into a source of comfort, and it carries the power of salvation, as faith comes by hearing the gospel. Pastor Travis challenged the church to move from a place of fear or complacency to bold, public witness, starting right where they are—in their homes, neighborhoods, and workplaces. He concluded with a call to surrender to Christ and to actively participate in the Great Commission, emphasizing that every believer has an obligation and the Holy Spirit's power to be a light in the world.
mainPoints: A genuine experience with Jesus creates an unstoppable compulsion to share your story, just as it did for the early disciples.
Your personal testimony holds the power to set people free, bring healing, and lead others to salvation.
Every believer is obligated and empowered by the Holy Spirit to be a public witness, starting right where they are.

versesMentioned.text: The priests and the captain of the temple guard and the Sadducees came up to Peter and John while they were speaking to the people. They were greatly disturbed because the apostles were teaching the people, proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection of the dead. They seized Peter and John, and because it was evening, they put them in jail until the next day. But many who heard the message believed; so the number of men who believed grew to about five thousand.
Then they called them in again and commanded them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
But Peter and John replied, 'Which is right in God’s eyes: to listen to you, or to him? You be the judges! As for us, we cannot help speaking about what we have seen and heard.'
But if I say, 'I will not mention his word or speak anymore in his name,' his word is in my heart like a fire, a fire shut up in my bones. I am weary of holding it in; indeed, I cannot.
How, then, can they call on the one they have not believed in? And how can they believe in the one of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching to them?
He said to them, 'Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.'
But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.

The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free.
Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
Jesus answered, 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'
Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.
who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes: first to the Jew, then to the Gentile.
Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of them when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled underfoot. You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

Monday.scripture: Acts 4:20 - 'As for us, we cannot help speaking about what we have seen and heard.'
Monday.action: Reflect on one specific way God has changed your life. Write it down in one sentence.
Monday.prayer: Lord, let the reality of what You've done in me be like a fire in my bones that I cannot keep to myself.

Tuesday.scripture: Acts 1:8 - 'You will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.'
Tuesday.action: Identify your 'Jerusalem'—your immediate circle (family, coworkers, neighbors). Pray for one person by name.
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me courage and an opportunity to be a witness for You right where I am today.

Wednesday.scripture: John 8:32 - 'Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.'
Wednesday.action: Consider a lie (fear, shame, insecurity) that once bound you. Acknowledge the truth of Christ's freedom in that area.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being the Truth that sets me free. Help me share this freedom with someone who feels bound.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 - 'He comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble.'
Thursday.action: Think of a past hurt where God comforted you. Be prepared to share that comfort if a similar situation arises in conversation.
Thursday.prayer: God, use my past pain as a source of Your comfort for others. Make me sensitive to those who are hurting.

Friday.scripture: Romans 1:16 - 'I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation.'
Friday.action: Do one small, public act that identifies you with Christ (e.g., pray before a meal in public, share a faith-based post online).
Friday.prayer: Father, replace any fear or shame in me with a bold confidence in the saving power of the gospel.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 5:14-16 - 'You are the light of the world... let your light shine before others.'
Saturday.action: Plan to invite someone to church or a small group. Send a simple, low-pressure text or message.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, You are the Light of the World. Help me not to hide Your light in me, but to shine it brightly for others to see.
